orgGate.label: Organisation
orgGate.help: Your organisation's short name (slug). You will be redirected to the right login page.
orgGate.errorNetwork: Unable to connect. Please try again.
orgGate.errorRateLimit: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment.

login.totpHelp: 6 digits from your authenticator app, or a backup code.

forgotPassword.successMessage: If an account is associated with this address, an email with reset instructions has just been sent.
forgotPassword.checkSpam: Remember to check your spam folder if you don't see it.
forgotPassword.formHelp: Enter the email associated with your account. You will receive a link to set a new password.

resetPassword.invalidLink: This link is invalid or expired. Request a new link from the login page.
resetPassword.successMessage: Your password has been updated. You can now sign in with your new password.

signup.planFreeDesc: 1 org, 1 user · personal vault + backups · permanently free, no trial
signup.planSharedDesc: 2 orgs, 5 users · multi-user, server management, OIDC · €5/month after 14-day trial
signup.planDedicatedDesc: 5 orgs, 15 users · dedicated subdomain + instance · €25/month after 14-day trial
signup.successProvisioned: Your workspace {slug} has been provisioned. A welcome email has been sent to {adminEmail}.

slugErrors.invalidFormat: Invalid format. Lowercase, alphanumeric and hyphens only.

extensionBanner.text: Install the Physalis extension to auto-fill your credentials directly from the browser.

page.title: Dashboard
page.noOrgHelp: Create one via the menu at the top left.

time.yearsAgo: {n, plural, one {# year ago} other {# years ago}}

billingCard.trialDaysLeft: {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}

trialBanner.expiredDescOwner: To continue using Physalis, subscribe to a plan.
trialBanner.expiredDescMember: Ask an OWNER to subscribe to a plan to continue.
trialBanner.activeTitle: Free trial — {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}
trialBanner.activeDescOwner: Subscribe before the trial ends to avoid interrupting your access.
trialBanner.activeDescMember: An OWNER can subscribe to secure long-term access.

extension.feature1: <bold>Auto-fill</bold> credentials on websites of Services and Accounts accessible from your projects
extension.feature2: <bold>Automatically save</bold> a new credential to your vault when you sign in to a site
extension.feature3: Access your personal vault and team vault credentials filtered by domain with one click
extension.stepChromeDevMode: Open chrome://extensions/ and enable <bold>Developer mode</bold> (toggle at the top right).
extension.stepChromeLoad: Click <bold>Load unpacked</bold> and select the unzipped folder, or drag the zip onto the window.
extension.stepFirefoxLoad: Click Load Temporary Add-on and select the manifest.json file from the unzipped folder.
extension.stepFirefoxNote: Note: on Firefox, the temporary installation is reset on next restart. For a permanent installation, wait for publication on addons.mozilla.org.
extension.stepOther: The extension is only available for Chrome (and Chromium-based browsers) and Firefox at the moment.
extension.tipContent: To pin the extension to the toolbar: click the <bold>🧩 Extensions</bold> icon in the browser toolbar, then click the <bold>📌 pin</bold> icon next to Physalis. The Physalis icon will then always be visible.

shares.mySharaesDesc: One-time links you send (encrypted via URL fragment).
shares.externalDesc: Invite a third party to share a secret with you (browser-side ECDH, no account required).
shares.noSharesHint: Click "📤 Create a share" at the top right to create your first one-time link.

createDialog.title: Create a share
createDialog.urlNotShownAgain: The URL will never be shown again.
createDialog.errorEmailFailed: Link created but email not sent: {error}.

shares.externalEmptyHint: Click "Allow an external share" to generate a link to send to a recipient.
shares.requestLinkOnce: For security reasons, the full link is only shown once (at creation). Recreate a request to get a new link.

revealDialog.title: Reveal secret
revealDialog.localHint: Never sent to the server. All decryption happens in this browser.
revealDialog.errorNotFound: Not yet submitted or not found.
revealDialog.error: Decryption failed. Invalid private key or wrong request?

createRequestDialog.title: Allow an external share
createRequestDialog.recipientHint: If filled, an email with the link is sent automatically. Otherwise copy manually after creation.

requestCreated.title: Request created
requestCreated.saveKeyWarning: ⚠️ Save this private key. It will never be shown again. Without it you cannot decrypt the received secret.

email.title: Email service
email.intro: Enable email sending for this client — one account shared by all projects and organizations. Once enabled, each project can connect its own domain.
email.notConfigured: Email service unavailable (not configured on the server).
email.statusEnabled: Service enabled. Projects can connect their domain.
email.readOnlyHint: Enabling requires OWNER/ADMIN of an organization.

backup.title: Automated backup
backup.intro: Enable automated database backups for your projects (encrypted, on one of your VPS). Per-project configuration is then done in each project.
backup.enableLabel: Enable automated backup for this client
backup.readOnlyHint: Enabling requires OWNER/ADMIN of an organization.
backup.destIntro: VPS and base path where backups are stored (shared by all this client's projects).
backup.destHint: Each project's effective path will be: <base path>/<project-slug>.

twoFactor.title: Two-factor authentication (TOTP)
twoFactor.enabled: ✅ Enabled. {count, plural, one {# backup code} other {# backup codes}} remaining.
twoFactor.disabled: Add a 2nd factor (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password…) in addition to your password for web login. Machine tokens are not affected.
twoFactor.step1: 1. Scan the QR code with your authenticator app
twoFactor.step2: 2. Enter the 6-digit code shown by your app to confirm:
twoFactor.disablePrompt: To disable, enter a current TOTP code or a backup code:
twoFactor.backupCodesHint: These 8 backup codes will never be shown again. Each code can be used once instead of a TOTP code (if you lose your phone).

userTokens.title: Integration tokens
userTokens.desc: Bearer tokens scoped to your account, for use in N8n workflows or custom scripts. Give read access to secrets, services and accounts of projects you are a member of. Different from machine tokens (scoped to a specific project+env in project settings).
userTokens.noTokens: No tokens. Create one to integrate an N8n workflow.
userTokens.saveNow: Copy this token now. It will never be shown again.
userTokens.revokeConfirm: Revoke token "{name}"? All integrations using it will stop working.

pluginSessions.title: Active plugin sessions
pluginSessions.desc: Sessions opened by the Physalis browser extension. A session lasts 4 hours by default. On expiry, the extension prompts for the TOTP code again. You can manually revoke any session (e.g. if you change device or lose one).

projects.noOrg: You are not a member of any organisation. Create one via the menu at the top left.
projects.emptyHint: Use the form above to create your first one.

createForm.title: Create a project
createForm.hint: 3 environments created automatically: production, staging, development.

access.servicesHelp: Third-party credentials (Stripe, OVH, hosting providers…) linked to this project.
access.accountsHelp: Test or service accounts used by the project's environments.

relTime.yearsAgo: {n, plural, one {# yr ago} other {# yr ago}}

projects.noEnvHint: Click ⚙ top right to create one.

redeploy.confirm: Trigger a GitHub Actions redeploy for environment {name}?

form.renameNote: The key will be renamed. External references (n8n, workflows…) using the old key must be updated.

import.title: Import .env — {env}
import.executeBtn: Import {count, plural, one {# secret} other {# secrets}}
import.entries: {count, plural, one {# entry} other {# entries}} read
import.updateCount: ↻ {count, plural, one {# to overwrite} other {# to overwrite}}
import.invalidCount: ⚠ {count, plural, one {# invalid name} other {# invalid names}}

rotation.dbUserNote: The user whose password will be changed.
rotation.jwtHint: A new secret will be automatically generated. Redeployment will be triggered via GitHub Actions (configured on the project).
rotation.fullDeployNote: Triggers the project build workflow (e.g. deploy.yml) instead of the simple redeploy (redeploy.yml). Enable if the value is injected at build time (e.g. VITE_*).
rotation.apiKeyHint: On each rotation, a new key is generated, the secret value is updated, the old key is immediately revoked, and a GitHub Actions redeploy is triggered so the application loads the new key.
rotation.apiKeyWarning: ⚠ Warning — Automatic rotation is only suitable if this key is loaded from the vault at application startup (via .env). If you copied it directly into n8n, Make or another external tool, you will need to update it manually after each rotation.
rotation.noApiConfigured: No API Gateway configured on this project. Create one in the API Gateway tab.
rotation.activeKeyNote: The key whose value is currently stored in this secret.

members.title: Project members
members.desc1: All organisation members see this project by default with a VIEWER role. You can hide the project for some, or elevate their role to EDITOR / OWNER.
members.desc2: Organisation OWNER/ADMIN always have implicit OWNER — not editable here.

tokens.title: Machine tokens — {env}
tokens.saveMsg: This token will only be shown once. Copy it now.

policies.title: OIDC Policies
policies.desc: Strict binding (repo · workflow · branch) → environment. No wildcards. The GitHub workflow must present an OIDC token whose claims exactly match a policy for /api/deploy to return the bundle.
policies.emptyHint: Without a policy, no GitHub workflow can fetch secrets via OIDC.
policies.repoRequired: Set the project's GitHub repo first (Settings).

compose.title: docker-compose — {env}
compose.placeholder:
# Paste your docker-compose.yml here
services:
  app:
    image: ...
compose.hint: The content is stored in plain text (PostgreSQL). Avoid hardcoding sensitive values — use ${VAR} and place values in the Secrets tab.

email.title: Email
email.intro: Configure the project's email sending. The domain and API key are injected into each environment's .env at deployment.
email.serviceDisabled: The email service is not enabled for this client. Enable it from the Security page (click your email in the header).
email.notConfigured: Email service unavailable (not configured on the server).
email.notConnected: No email domain connected for this project.
email.dnsHint: Add these records at your registrar, then run verification.
email.verifyFail: Verification incomplete. Check your DNS records.
email.disconnectConfirm: Disconnect the project's email? The API key will be revoked.
email.readOnlyHint: Connecting requires EDITOR role or higher.
email.usageAlert: Quota almost reached (≥ 80%). Beyond it, sends are blocked.

send.noSenders: First add an authorized sender in the Senders tab.

vars.title: Environment variables
vars.hint: These variables are injected into each environment's .env at deployment.

rotation.title: Automatic rotation
rotation.lastError: The last rotation failed. A retry is scheduled.
rotation.hint: Blue/green rotation: a new key is generated and a redeploy triggered; the old key is only revoked at the next cycle (giving environments time to redeploy).

backup.title: Automated backup
backup.intro: Automatic, encrypted backup of the project's database to one of your VPS.
backup.readOnlyHint: Configuration requires EDITOR role or higher.
backup.gpgNote: Physalis will generate a dedicated GPG keypair (private key kept on your VPS). The config takes effect once the agent is deployed.
backup.historyEmpty: No backups yet (the agent is not deployed yet).
backup.detectNote: Databases detected from docker-compose + .env — review and fix if needed.
backup.noDbs: No database. Add one or run detection.
backup.scheduleExample: E.g. a backup every day at 3:00 UTC. Retention: keep 7 daily, 4 weekly and 3 monthly backups.
backup.backupRunning: Backup running… (may take 1-2 min, the page refreshes automatically)

settings.title: Project settings
settings.slugWarning: ⚠ Changing the slug breaks scripts that hardcode the URL /api/secrets/{slug}/<env>. The machine token remains valid, only the URL changes.
settings.githubNote: The Redeploy button triggers a workflow_dispatch on this workflow. The GitHub PAT must be stored in the org secret GITHUB_DISPATCH_TOKEN.
settings.envDeleteConfirm:
Delete environment "{name}"?

This will delete {count, plural, one {# secret} other {# secrets}} and all machine tokens for this environment (cascade).
settings.slugChangeConfirm:
⚠ You are changing the project slug from "{old}" to "{new}".

Consequences:
• Scripts using the Bearer machine URL /api/secrets/{old}/<env> will return 403 until updated with the new slug.
• Bookmarks /projects/{old} will no longer work.

No need to regenerate machine tokens (they remain valid) or touch the GitHub PAT. Continue?

gateway.revokeKeyConfirm: Revoke this key? It will be immediately invalidated.
gateway.deleteApiConfirm: Delete API "{name}" and all its keys?
gateway.subtitle: Protect your APIs with keys managed by Physalis.
gateway.emptyHint: Create an API to start protecting your endpoints.
gateway.keyCount: {count, plural, one {# key} other {# keys}} — {logs} req.
gateway.copyKeyNow: Copy this key now — it won't be shown again:
gateway.noKeys: No keys. Create one to get started.
gateway.createdKeysNote: These keys are shown once only. They have been stored as secrets in the selected environments — you can find them in the Secrets tab.
gateway.autoGenerateNote: Automatically generate keys and store them as secrets in the project environments.
gateway.secretNameNote: Secret key created in the selected environments (e.g.: {example}).

audit.adminDesc: History of all sensitive actions in the organisation.
audit.memberDesc: Your actions on projects you have access to.

members.title: Members
members.inviteInAppNote: The invitation will be immediately visible on the recipient's dashboard (TTL 48h).
members.inviteEmailNote: An email with an acceptance link valid for 48h will be sent.
members.removeConfirm:
Remove {email} from the organisation?

Machine tokens created by this member will be revoked.
members.expiredNote: The invitation link has expired — use "Resend" to generate a new one
members.resendNoteInApp: Regenerates the link and extends expiry (in-app, no email)
members.resendNoteEmail: Regenerates the link, extends expiry and resends the email

tokens.title: Integration tokens
tokens.desc: Bearer tokens scoped to the organisation, for N8n / script integrations. Different from user tokens (Settings → Security) because they survive the creator leaving. Read-only in V1. Global org secrets (GITHUB_DISPATCH_TOKEN etc.) are never accessible via these tokens — by design.
tokens.devModeNote: ℹ️ DEV mode: you can create your own tokens with automatic restrictions — projects limited to those you are a member of, mandatory expiry ≤ {days} days, max {max} active tokens. You only see your own tokens. ADMINs see all org tokens.
tokens.emptyHint: Create a token to let N8n / scripts access the organisation's credentials.

form.title: New integration token
form.scopeProjectsDesc: Required for dynamic loading in the N8n node.
form.projectsHint: Select only the required projects. Recommended for the principle of least privilege.
form.allProjects: ⚠ All current and future projects of the organisation
form.allProjectsConfirm:
Enabling "all current and future projects" gives access to all projects in the organisation, including those created later. Use with caution.

Confirm?
form.allProjectsNote: The "all projects" option is reserved for ADMINs. You can only select projects you are a member of.

tokens.saveNow: Copy this token now. It will never be shown again.
tokens.regenerateConfirm:
Regenerate token "{name}"?

The current secret will be immediately invalidated. Update your integrations (N8n, scripts) with the new value.

Scopes, projects and expiry are preserved.
tokens.revokeConfirm: Revoke token "{name}"? All integrations using it will stop working.
tokens.saveToVaultTitle: Creates an entry in your personal vault with this token as the password. You can then move it to a team collection to share it.
tokens.n8nConfigNote: N8n configuration: Vault URL = your Physalis instance, Token = this token.

secrets.title: Global organisation secrets
secrets.desc: Read by vault features. Reserved keys:
secrets.reserved2: REGISTRY_PAT + REGISTRY_USER — exposed in /api/deploy bundle under registry.{user,pat} (scope read:packages)

form.expiryHint: Manual reminder: Physalis does not know a token's real expiry. Set it again on each renewal.

servers.readOnly: Reserved for DEV/ADMIN/OWNER of the organisation.
servers.panelDesc: Target VPS for OIDC deployments. One private SSH key per server — the key is encrypted at rest and never re-read after creation (rotation = new server).
servers.envLinked: {count, plural, one {# env} other {# envs}} linked
servers.sshKeyLabel: Private SSH key (PEM/OpenSSH, pasted as plaintext, will be AES-256-GCM encrypted)
servers.sshKeyWarning: ⚠ The key will never be readable in the UI after creation. Keep it in your local manager until you validate that the deployment works, then delete it.
servers.rotationNote: To rotate the SSH key: delete and recreate the server.

rotation.title: Secret rotation
rotation.desc: Secrets with rotation enabled, grouped by project. Automatic rotations are triggered by the cron configured on your instance.
rotation.confirmManualTitle: Confirm that manual rotation has been performed
rotation.forceTitle: Trigger rotation immediately, outside the normal cycle

relTime.yearsAgo: {n, plural, one {# yr ago} other {# yr ago}}

settings.rotationDesc: Enables the Rotation and Databases tabs. Ongoing rotations are paused if disabled.
settings.deleteDesc: Deleting the organisation deletes everything: projects, secrets, machine tokens, members, invitations. This action is irreversible.
settings.deleteConfirm: This action is IRREVERSIBLE. All projects, secrets, machine tokens, members and invitations of the organisation will be deleted. Continue?

roleInfo.dialogDesc: Organisation roles control access to global secrets, servers and projects. From least to most permissive:

member.title: Member
member.desc: Basic access. Does NOT see any projects by default — the admin must explicitly add them as ProjectMember (with VIEWER/EDITOR/OWNER role) on each project they need to access.
member.viewProjects: View projects (only those with explicit ProjectMember)

dev.title: Dev
dev.desc: Developer profile. Implicit EDITOR on all org projects (CRUD project-level secrets, redeploy). Read global secrets and servers. Can view their own actions in the audit log.
dev.implicitEditor: Implicit EDITOR on all org projects
dev.ownAudit: Audit log: own actions on accessible projects

admin_dev.title: AdminDev
admin_dev.desc: Developer with enhanced rights: all Dev rights + full CRUD on org-global secrets and servers. Cannot manage members or delete the org.
admin_dev.implicitEditor: Implicit EDITOR on all org projects
admin_dev.ownAudit: Audit log: own actions on accessible projects

admin.title: Admin
admin.desc: Manages the organisation day-to-day. Implicit OWNER on all projects, can add/remove members (except granting OWNER).
admin.implicitOwner: Implicit OWNER on all org projects

owner.title: Owner
owner.desc: Full control over the organisation, including destructive actions.

audit.adminDesc: History of all sensitive actions in the organisation.
audit.memberDesc: Your actions on projects you have access to.

account.noTenantHint: This page is only available in a tenant.
account.trialDaysLeft: {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}
account.ownerOnly: Only an owner (OWNER) can subscribe or manage billing.
account.addonNote: ({base} included + {extra, plural, one {# add-on} other {# add-ons}} · {price}/month)
account.addonsUpdated: Add-ons updated and payment recorded. The invoice will be available in Stripe.
account.addonsTrialNote: Add-ons are available after subscribing. Subscribe first, then you can add or remove add-ons at any time.
account.billingFreeNote: Permanently free plan. To unlock more orgs and members, upgrade to SHARED or DEDICATED.
account.billingCompedNote: This subscription is graciously offered — no charge.
account.billingExtraOrgs: {n, plural, one {# extra organisation} other {# extra organisations}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraUsers: {n, plural, one {# extra user} other {# extra users}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraServers: {n, plural, one {# extra server} other {# extra servers}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraOidcProjects: {n, plural, one {# extra OIDC project} other {# extra OIDC projects}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraEmail5k: {n, plural, one {# +5,000 emails pack} other {# +5,000 emails packs}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraEmail15k: {n, plural, one {# +15,000 emails pack} other {# +15,000 emails packs}} ({price} each)
account.billingExtraEmail30k: {n, plural, one {# +30,000 emails pack} other {# +30,000 emails packs}} ({price} each)
account.subscribeConsent: By subscribing, you agree to our <terms>terms of service</terms> and our <privacy>privacy policy</privacy>.

downgrade.warning: Warning! Your current usage exceeds the FREE plan quotas. After downgrade:
downgrade.currentUsage: You are currently using {orgsCount, plural, one {# organisation} other {# organisations}} and {usersCount, plural, one {# user} other {# users}}.
downgrade.freeLimits: The FREE plan includes 1 organisation and 1 user.
downgrade.dataNote: Your existing data remains intact. You can still view and edit it, and everything will be reactivated immediately if you re-subscribe.
downgrade.withinQuota: Your usage fits within the FREE quotas. No restrictions after switching.
downgrade.cancelNote: Your paid subscription will be cancelled immediately. You will no longer be billed.

account.previewNote: The new plan is applied immediately. Stripe bills the pro-rata for the remaining days of the current cycle.

export.desc: Download all your personal data (orgs, projects, decrypted secrets, vault, audit) in JSON format. Compliant with Article 20 GDPR (right to data portability).
export.descPersonal: Download only your personal vault (decrypted password entries) in JSON format. Compliant with Article 20 GDPR (right to data portability).

vault.pageSubtitle: Your private credentials. No sharing, visible only to you.
vault.noCollections: No collections. Create one to organise your entries.

deleteCollection.confirm: Delete collection "{name}"? {count, plural, one {# entry} other {# entries}} will return to "Uncategorised".

import.title: Import CSV
import.formats: Supported formats: Bitwarden, Chrome, generic CSV. Passwords are encrypted server-side before storage. Entries are created in this collection; the folder column (if present) is preserved as a tag.
import.preview: {count, plural, one {# entry} other {# entries}} ready to import.
import.submitBtn: Import {count, plural, one {# secret} other {# secrets}}
import.success: ✓ {count, plural, one {# entry imported} other {# entries imported}}

teamVault.scopeOrg: Team vaults at organisation level. Members managed by collection (OWNER/EDITOR/VIEWER). OrgADMIN+ have implicit OWNER access, OrgDEV has implicit EDITOR access on all collections.
teamVault.scopeProject: Team vaults at project level. Rights inherited from project RBAC (project VIEWER/EDITOR/OWNER → same role on collections).
teamVault.noCollectionsCreate: Click "+ New collection" to create one.
teamVault.deleteConfirm: Delete collection "{name}" and all its entries?
teamVault.emptyMembers: No explicit members. OrgADMIN+ have implicit OWNER access, OrgDEV has implicit EDITOR access on all collections.

relTime.yearsAgo: {n, plural, one {# yr ago} other {# yr ago}}

onboarding.welcomeDesc: Store your private credentials here (Gmail, Netflix, etc.). No sharing, visible only to you.
onboarding.importDesc: Migrate your existing vault in seconds (CSV).
onboarding.extensionDesc: Auto-fill, generation, save-prompt on your sites.

form.totpHint: Optional. Paste the base32 secret shown next to the QR code, or the full otpauth:// URL. The extension will display the 6-digit code in real time.
form.moveCollectionHint: To move this entry to another collection in the same scope.

move.teamVaultNote: To a team collection. The entry will leave your personal vault and be accessible to the collection's members.
move.noEditorCollections: No EDITOR+ collections here. Create one from the Vault tab.

overageBanner.title: FREE plan over quota
overageBanner.desc: Your usage exceeds the FREE plan quotas:
overageBanner.hint: To reactivate: reduce usage to 1 org / 1 user, or subscribe to a paid plan.

paymentBanner.title: Last payment failed
paymentBanner.descOwner: Your card was declined on the last payment. Stripe will automatically retry, but we recommend updating your payment method now to avoid service suspension.
paymentBanner.descMember: The last payment failed. Ask an OWNER to update the payment method to avoid service suspension.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Physalis usage guides. Choose a section below or via the sidebar.

common.ignore: If you did not expect this, ignore this message.
common.ignore_invitation: If you were not expecting this invitation, ignore this message.
common.ignore_reset: If you did not request this, ignore this message — your current password remains valid.
common.linkExpires: This link expires on {date} and can only be used once.

invitation.subject: Invitation to join {org} on Physalis
invitation.title: Invitation to join {org}
invitation.body: {inviter} invites you to join the organisation {org} on Physalis.

welcome.subject: Welcome to Physalis — {client}
welcome.title: Welcome to Physalis
welcome.body: The account {client} has been created successfully (plan: {plan}).
welcome.trialFree: Your plan is permanently free — no expiry.

passwordReset.subject: Reset your Physalis password
passwordReset.title: Password reset
passwordReset.body: You (or someone else) requested a password reset for this Physalis account.

secretRequest.subject: {requester} is requesting a secret from you
secretRequest.title: Secure secret request
secretRequest.body: {requester} (via Physalis) is asking you to share:
secretRequest.encrypted: 🔐 The secret is encrypted in your browser before sending — Physalis cannot read it.

secretReceived.subject: Secret received: {label}
secretReceived.title: Secret received
secretReceived.body: The secret requested for {label} has just been submitted on Physalis.
secretReceived.warning: If the timing seems unexpected, revoke the request without revealing it.

shareConsumed.subject: Share "{label}" consumed
shareConsumed.title: Share consumed
shareConsumed.body: Your share "{label}" has just been opened on Physalis.
shareConsumed.warning: If this was not you (or the intended recipient), consider the content compromised and change it if needed.

checkoutCompleted.subject: Welcome to Physalis {plan} — {client}
checkoutCompleted.title: Subscription activated
checkoutCompleted.body: Your Physalis {plan} subscription is now active for {client}.
checkoutCompleted.stripeNote: Stripe has sent you separately the detailed payment receipt with the exact amount and your invoice download link.

downgradeFree.subject: Switch to FREE plan confirmed — {client}
downgradeFree.title: Switch to FREE plan confirmed
downgradeFree.body: Your Physalis subscription for {client} has been switched to the FREE plan. Your card will no longer be charged.
downgradeFree.noOverage: Your usage fits within the FREE quotas. No restrictions.
downgradeFree.overageItem1: Creation of new organisations / users blocked.
downgradeFree.overageItem2: Automatic deployments disabled on excess organisations (main organisation remains allowed).
downgradeFree.overageNote: Your data is preserved. Re-subscribe at any time to reactivate everything.

overageReminder.subjectJ7: Your Physalis resources are waiting for you
overageReminder.subjectJ30: Final reminder — your data is still there
overageReminder.leadJ7: It has been one week since your {client} account has been in overage on the FREE plan.
overageReminder.leadJ30: It has been one month since your {client} account has been in overage on the FREE plan.
overageReminder.preserved: All your data is preserved and accessible. However:
overageReminder.item2: Automatic deployments are disabled on excess organisations.

invite.wrongWorkspace: This invitation must be opened from your workspace URL (e.g. myworkspace.physalis.cloud/invite/...).

errors.alreadyAccepted: This invitation has already been accepted.
errors.notFound: This invitation could not be found.

invite.invitationLine: {inviter} invites you to join {organization} with the role {role}.
invite.signInToAccept: Sign in with the address {email} to accept.
invite.emailMismatch:
This invitation was sent to {invitedEmail}. You are signed in as {currentEmail}.

Sign out and sign back in with the correct address.

secretRequest.linkInvalid: This link is no longer valid. It may have expired or already been used. Contact the person who sent it to you to get a new link.

errors.linkGone: This link is no longer valid (already used, expired or revoked).
errors.rateLimit: Too many attempts, try again in an hour.
errors.encryptFailed: Unable to encrypt the secret. Make sure your browser supports WebCrypto.

secretRequest.successMessage: Your secret has been securely sent to {email}.
secretRequest.successNote: This link is now invalid. You can close this page.
secretRequest.encryptionNotice: 🔐 Your secret is encrypted in your browser before transmission. Physalis cannot read it.
